title: Property Management Operations Score
subtitle: Find the exact workflows where your team is losing hours every week

persona_selector.text: How many units does your company currently manage?

answers.label: Under 200 units
200 to 750 units
750 to 2,000 units
Over 2,000 units

questions.text: How are inbound tenant requests (maintenance, questions, complaints) captured today?

answers.label: Phone calls and personal emails, no central log
Shared inbox, manually sorted by staff
Portal exists but tenants still default to calling or texting
Portal is the main channel, staff triage daily
Central intake across all channels with automated routing

questions.text: When a tenant submits a request, how fast does someone respond?

answers.label: Often more than 24 hours, depends on workload
Within a business day, but inconsistent
Within 4 hours during business hours
Within 1 hour during business hours, automated acknowledgment after
Instant automated reply plus human follow-up under 1 hour

questions.text: How much of your tenant communication is repeated boilerplate (rent reminders, lease renewals, policy notices)?

answers.label: Almost all sent manually, one by one
Some templates, still sent manually
Scheduled email blasts for the big ones
Most recurring comms are automated with merge fields
Fully automated across email, SMS, and portal with logic-based triggers

questions.text: Can you pull a clean history of every interaction with a specific tenant in under 60 seconds?

answers.label: No, we'd have to dig through inboxes and notes
Partially, some in the system and some in email
Yes for portal messages, not for calls or SMS
Yes across channels, minor gaps
Yes, complete timeline in one place

questions.text: How often do requests slip through the cracks or get answered twice by different staff?

answers.label: Weekly, it's a known problem
A few times a month
Occasionally, usually on busy days
Rarely, we have assignment rules
Almost never, the system enforces ownership

low.headline: Your inbox is running your business
low.text: Right now tenant requests are scattered across phone, email, text, and memory. That's eating hours every day and letting issues slip. The fastest win here is a single intake channel that auto-logs and auto-acknowledges every request.
steps: Pick one central intake channel (portal or shared inbox) and tell every tenant to use it
Set up an automated acknowledgment reply so tenants know you got it
Assign a single owner per request type so nothing sits unclaimed

mid.headline: Good foundation, leaking at the edges
mid.text: You have systems, but manual handling is still pulling staff time on repeat comms and status updates. The opportunity is automating the recurring 80 percent so your team only touches the exceptions.
steps: Audit every comm you send more than 10 times a month and templatize it
Automate rent reminders, renewal notices, and maintenance status updates end-to-end
Build a tenant timeline view that pulls email, SMS, and portal into one record

high.headline: You're running a tight comms operation
high.text: Your team is not losing meaningful hours here. Next level is using the data you're already capturing to get predictive on tenant issues before they escalate.
steps: Layer sentiment analysis on inbound messages to flag at-risk tenants early
Use response time and resolution data to spot overloaded staff or property types
Build a self-service AI assistant for common tenant questions to deflect routine volume

questions.text: How are new leases created and sent for signature?

answers.label: Word doc edited manually, emailed as PDF
Template doc with manual field editing
E-signature tool, fields filled by hand
E-signature with data pulled from our system
Fully automated generation and send triggered by application approval

questions.text: How do you track lease renewal timing and outreach?

answers.label: Spreadsheet someone checks when they remember
Calendar reminders per property manager
Report pulled monthly from the PM software
Automated alerts 90/60/30 days out to the right staff
Automated outreach sequence to the tenant at each window

questions.text: Where do signed leases and tenant documents live?

answers.label: Email inboxes and random drive folders
Shared drive, inconsistent naming
Central drive with a folder structure we mostly follow
PM software or DMS with tagged documents
Central system with auto-tagging, search, and retention rules

questions.text: How are compliance documents (insurance, addendums, disclosures) tracked for expiration?

answers.label: Not really tracked, we find out when there's a problem
Someone owns a spreadsheet of expirations
Calendar reminders per document
System flags expiring docs and emails the owner
Automated expiration tracking plus auto-request for renewals

low.headline: Document drift is costing you money
low.text: Manual lease handling is slowing your time-to-sign and creating compliance exposure. Every lost day between approval and signature is a day of lost rent. A templated generation and e-sign flow usually pays back in weeks.
steps: Move every lease type to an e-signature tool with pre-filled fields
Create a single document vault with a naming convention staff actually use
Build a simple expiration tracker for insurance and compliance docs

mid.headline: The plumbing works, the automation doesn't
mid.text: You've digitized the basics but renewals and compliance still rely on humans remembering. Automating the reminder and outreach layer is where you claw back hours and reduce risk.
steps: Trigger renewal outreach automatically at 90, 60, and 30 days
Auto-generate leases from approved application data, no retyping
Build an expiration dashboard that escalates if a document isn't renewed in time

high.headline: Document ops is a competitive advantage for you
high.text: Your lease and document flow is already efficient. The next level is using what's in those documents to drive portfolio insights and renewal strategy.
steps: Extract structured data from every lease into a single source of truth
Use renewal data to model which tenants and units are at highest churn risk
Automate personalized renewal offers based on tenant history and market rent

questions.text: How do maintenance requests get routed to the right vendor or tech?

answers.label: Office manager reads each one and decides who to call
Simple rules based on property, still manual dispatch
PM software assigns based on property and category
Automated routing by property, category, and vendor availability
Smart routing with vendor ratings, SLAs, and load balancing

questions.text: How do you track vendor response and completion times?

answers.label: We don't, we find out when the tenant complains
Someone spot checks when problems come up
Manual reports pulled occasionally
Dashboard with response and completion times per vendor
Automated SLA tracking with alerts when vendors slip

questions.text: How are tenants updated on work order status?

answers.label: They aren't, unless they call us
Staff updates them manually when they remember
Update sent when work order is closed
Automated updates at assigned, in-progress, and closed
Real-time status plus proactive comms when timelines slip

questions.text: How do invoices from vendors get captured and approved?

answers.label: Paper or PDF invoices, manually entered into accounting
Emailed PDFs, entered by hand, approved over email
Vendor portal with manual approval workflow
Auto-capture from email or portal, routed for approval
Auto-capture, auto-match to work order, approval and payment workflow

low.headline: Maintenance is your biggest hidden time sink
low.text: Manual routing and zero vendor accountability is where most PM companies lose the biggest chunk of hours. Tenants complain more, vendors drift, and your office manager becomes a full-time dispatcher. Fix this first.
steps: Document your vendor list with categories, coverage areas, and rates in one place
Set up automated routing rules for the top 3 request categories by volume
Require vendors to update work order status in your system, not by phone

mid.headline: You route well, you measure poorly
mid.text: Assignments happen, but you're not closing the loop on vendor performance or tenant communication. That's where trust erodes and repeat complaints come from. Layer tracking and automated status updates on top of what you have.
steps: Build a vendor scorecard covering response time, completion time, and rework rate
Automate tenant status updates at every work order state change
Auto-capture vendor invoices and match them to the originating work order

high.headline: Maintenance is dialed in
high.text: Your team handles volume without drowning. Next level is predictive maintenance and AI-driven vendor optimization so you reduce reactive work entirely.
steps: Use historical data to predict unit-level maintenance needs and schedule proactively
Optimize vendor assignments with AI based on cost, speed, and quality together
Give tenants a self-service AI assistant for common maintenance questions

questions.text: How is rent collected today?

answers.label: Mostly checks and some ACH, manually recorded
Online payment portal, some manual entry
Portal is the default, checks are exceptions
Portal with auto-pay encouraged, auto-reconciled
Auto-pay default, full auto-reconciliation, late fees automated

questions.text: How are delinquencies handled?

answers.label: Staff manually reviews and sends individual notices
Weekly report, staff sends templated emails
Automated reminders, manual escalation
Full automated delinquency sequence with escalation tiers
Automated sequence plus AI-assisted collections outreach

questions.text: How long does month-end close take?

answers.label: Two weeks or more, always painful
About a week, lots of manual fixes
3 to 5 days
1 to 2 days
Same day, close is largely automated

questions.text: How do owners receive financial statements and updates?

answers.label: Manually built statements, emailed monthly
Templated statements, still manually sent
Owner portal, statements posted monthly
Owner portal with auto-generated monthly statements
Real-time owner dashboard plus automated monthly reports

low.headline: Money is moving slowly through your business
low.text: Manual rent handling and a painful close process means cash visibility lags reality by weeks. That affects every decision you make. Start by moving collections and reconciliation into one flow.
steps: Make online payment the default for every tenant, with auto-pay incentives
Build a templated delinquency sequence tied to days past due
Reconcile bank activity daily instead of in a month-end sprint

mid.headline: Collections work, reporting lags
mid.text: Your front-end collections are solid, but close and owner reporting still eat days of staff time. Automating statement generation and owner comms is a fast, visible win.
steps: Automate monthly owner statements with standardized formatting
Cut month-end close by automating recurring journal entries and reconciliations
Add a delinquency escalation workflow with legal-step triggers at set thresholds

high.headline: Your accounting ops punches above its weight
high.text: You're already fast and clean here. Next level is real-time financial intelligence and portfolio-level insight for you and your owners.
steps: Build a real-time owner dashboard with key performance indicators
Forecast cash flow and delinquency risk using historical data and AI
Benchmark property and portfolio performance against peer data

questions.text: How do vacant units get listed across channels?

answers.label: Staff posts manually to each site
Syndication tool, staff triggers it per listing
Automated syndication when a unit is marked vacant
Auto-syndication plus auto-refresh and repricing rules
Full auto-syndication, dynamic pricing, AI-written listing copy

questions.text: How are leads followed up after an inquiry?

answers.label: Staff responds when they have time, often hours later
Templated replies, manually sent
Auto-reply with next steps, staff follow-up within a day
Automated nurture sequence plus live response under an hour
AI leasing assistant handles qualification and booking end-to-end

questions.text: How are showings scheduled?

answers.label: Back-and-forth email or phone calls
Staff coordinates via shared calendar
Self-scheduling link for the leasing agent
Self-scheduling with auto-routing by unit and availability
Self-showings with smart lock access plus automated pre and post comms

questions.text: How is the application and screening process run?

answers.label: Paper or PDF forms, manual screening calls
Online application, manual screening steps
Online application with integrated screening
Online application, automated screening, auto-notifications
End-to-end automated from application to lease generation

low.headline: Your leasing funnel is leaking
low.text: Slow response times and manual listing work means you're losing qualified leads to competitors who respond in minutes. Every day a unit sits vacant is rent you don't get back. Tighten the top of the funnel first.
steps: Set up automatic listing syndication triggered by unit vacancy
Add an auto-reply to every inquiry with a showing scheduling link
Move applications fully online with integrated screening

mid.headline: Solid leasing ops, slow in the gaps
mid.text: You're capturing leads but losing hours on coordination and follow-up. Shifting to self-scheduling and automated nurture gets your agents focused on closing, not logistics.
steps: Deploy self-scheduling for every showing with auto-routing
Build a 7 to 14 day automated nurture sequence for leads who don't book
Automate the handoff from approved application to lease generation

high.headline: Leasing is a machine
high.text: You're already efficient from listing to lease. Next level is AI-driven leasing assistants and dynamic pricing to maximize revenue per unit.
steps: Deploy an AI leasing assistant that qualifies and books 24/7
Use dynamic pricing based on demand, season, and competitor data
Add self-showing technology to expand showing windows without staff cost

questions.text: How many different software tools does your team use daily to run operations?

answers.label: 6 or more, and they don't talk to each other
4 to 5, some integrations, lots of copy-paste
3 to 4 core tools with basic integrations
3 to 4 tools with solid integrations, minimal duplicate entry
Consolidated stack, fully integrated, single source of truth

questions.text: How often is data re-entered manually between systems?

answers.label: Constantly, it's a big part of someone's job
Daily, on several workflows
Weekly, on a few reports
Rarely, only for edge cases
Never, everything syncs automatically

questions.text: Can you pull a real-time view of portfolio performance (occupancy, delinquency, maintenance load) without waiting on a report?

answers.label: No, reports take days and are always behind
Partially, some metrics are current, others aren't
Yes for core metrics, on request
Yes via a dashboard updated daily
Yes, real-time dashboard for owners and staff

questions.text: When a new property is onboarded, how long does it take to be fully set up in all your systems?

answers.label: Weeks, lots of manual setup
About a week
3 to 5 days
1 to 2 days, mostly automated
Same day, onboarding is a workflow not a project

low.headline: You have a systems problem, not a staffing problem
low.text: Your team is doing heroic work patching over broken integrations. Every duplicate entry is a chance for errors and wasted hours. Start by mapping your data flows and fixing the worst 2 or 3 handoffs.
steps: Map every place data is copied between systems and rank by frequency
Integrate your top 2 highest-volume handoffs first
Eliminate at least one tool that overlaps with another in your stack

mid.headline: Connected but not unified
mid.text: Your tools talk, but not well enough to give you real-time visibility. The payoff here is huge because a unified data layer powers everything else (reporting, automation, AI).
steps: Pick one system as the source of truth and push data from there
Build a real-time operations dashboard for leadership
Automate new property onboarding as a single repeatable workflow

high.headline: Your data foundation is a strategic asset
high.text: You already have integrated systems and live visibility. Next level is using that data with AI to predict issues and automate decisions, not just workflows.
steps: Layer predictive analytics on occupancy, delinquency, and maintenance
Use AI agents to monitor data in real time and flag anomalies
Productize your data into owner-facing insights as a differentiator

cta.headline: Turn your score into a 90-day operations plan
cta.description: Book a 30-minute strategy call and I'll walk through your score with you, pinpoint the 3 automations that will give you the biggest hours-back-per-week return, and map a realistic 90-day plan to ship them. No pitch, just a working session.
